# Springer LNCS LaTeX Template

Lecture Notes in Computer Science

Template for Springer LNCS, LNAI, and LNBI proceedings. Used by many computer science conferences.

- [Official Page](https://www.springer.com/gp/computer-science/lncs/conference-proceedings-guidelines)
- [Download](https://www.springer.com/gp/computer-science/lncs/conference-proceedings-guidelines)
- [Open in Overleaf](https://www.overleaf.com/latex/templates/springer-lecture-notes-in-computer-science/kzwwpvhwnvfj)

![Springer LNCS LaTeX template preview](https://www.underleaf.ai/templates/og/springer-lncs.png)

## About the Springer LNCS Template

The Springer LNCS (Lecture Notes in Computer Science) template is used by a vast number of computer science conferences that publish their proceedings through Springer. This includes ECCV, MICCAI, and hundreds of other venues in the LNCS, LNAI (Artificial Intelligence), and LNBI (Bioinformatics) series.

The llncs document class produces a single-column layout on A4 paper. Page limits typically range from 12-15 pages depending on the specific conference. The template is maintained by Springer and available from their official conference proceedings guidelines page.

The current version (llncs.cls v2.24) is available on CTAN and pre-loaded in Overleaf. When using Overleaf, it's recommended to switch to TeXLive 2025 or later for the best compatibility. An enhanced community version on GitHub adds modern features like microtype and hyperref while maintaining full compatibility with the official template.

## Formatting Requirements

| Property | Specification |
| --- | --- |
| Layout | Single column |
| Font Size | 10pt |
| Paper Size | A4 |
| Document Class | `llncs` |
| Citation Style | Numbered, Springer style |
| Page Limit | Usually 12-15 pages |

Springer

LNCS

Proceedings

Usually 12-15 pages

## Frequently Asked Questions

What is the difference between LNCS, LNAI, and LNBI?

They are different series within Springer's Lecture Notes: LNCS (Computer Science), LNAI (Artificial Intelligence), and LNBI (Bioinformatics). All use the same llncs template.

What is the typical LNCS page limit?

Page limits vary by conference but are typically 12-15 pages for full papers. Check your specific conference's call for papers for exact limits.

Does LNCS use A4 or US Letter paper?

LNCS uses A4 paper size, unlike many US-based conferences that use US Letter. The llncs class handles this automatically.

# Springer Lecture Notes in Computer Science

[Open as Template](https://www.overleaf.com/project/new/template/34930?id=302536&latexEngine=pdflatex&mainFile=samplepaper.tex&templateName=Springer+Lecture+Notes+in+Computer+Science&texImage=texlive-full%3A2025.1) View Source [View PDF](https://www.overleaf.com/latex/templates/springer-lecture-notes-in-computer-science/kzwwpvhwnvfj.pdf)

**Author:**

From [the LNCS guidelines](http://www.springer.com/computer/lncs/lncs+authors?SGWID=0-40209-0-0-0).

**Last Updated:**

2 years ago

**License:**

Other (as stated in the work)

**Abstract:**

Springer has provided LaTeX style files and example articles to help you prepare your paper for submission to their journals.

This template is for contributions to the Springer Lecture Notes in Computer Science (LNCS) , based on the LaTeX2e+Proceedings+Templates+download.zip file from their [author guidance](http://www.springer.com/computer/lncs/lncs+authors?SGWID=0-40209-0-0-0) page, with `llncs.cls` v2.24 (29-Jan-2024).

The style files and example paper are pre-loaded into Overleaf as a template - you can now create an LNCS-formatted document for editing online simply by clicking the image above.

For further information, please see their [Information for LNCS Authors](http://www.springer.com/computer/lncs/lncs+authors?SGWID=0-40209-0-0-0) page, where you will find guidelines and technical instructions for the preparation of contributions.

**Tags:**

[Springer](https://www.overleaf.com/gallery/tagged/springer) [Journal articles](https://www.overleaf.com/gallery/tagged/academic-journal)
